title: Ipomoea dalzellii
intro: Ipomoea dalzellii, also known as Dalzell's morning glory, is a species of flowering plant in the Convolvulaceae family. The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues, due to its reported health benefits of reducing oxidative stress and promoting immune system function. The bioactive constituents of Ipomoea dalzellii include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Ipomoea dalzellii is commonly prepared as a decoction, infusion, or powder, and is used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and sore throat.
taxonomy: Ipomoea dalzellii is classified within the family Convolvulaceae. This family is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as bindweeds or morning glories. The genus Ipomoea is a subset of the Convolvulaceae family and is native to the Americas, Africa, and Asia. Within the genus Ipomoea, I. dalzellii is a species of flowering plant. The classification of I. dalzellii is further divided into its binomial nomenclature, consisting of the genus and species names.
